title: Password Generator
metaDescription: Generate cryptographically secure random passwords in your browser using the Web Crypto API. Configurable length and character sets. Nothing is sent to any server.
shortDescription: Generate secure random passwords with configurable length and character rules.

guideSections.heading: Why Length Matters More Than Complexity Rules
guideSections.body: A 16-character password using only lowercase letters has more possible combinations than an 8-character password using every character type available. Entropy scales with length far faster than with character set size, which is why modern guidance from NIST favors longer passphrases over short passwords forced into arbitrary complexity rules.

guideSections.heading: What 'Cryptographically Secure' Actually Means Here
guideSections.body: This generator sources randomness from the Web Crypto API's getRandomValues() method, the same randomness source used for cryptographic keys, rather than Math.random(), which is explicitly not designed to be unpredictable and should never be used for anything security-related. The difference is invisible in the output but matters entirely in how guessable the result is to an attacker.

guideSections.heading: Why Excluding Similar Characters Is a Usability Tradeoff, Not a Security One
guideSections.body: Removing visually similar characters like 0, O, 1, l, and I makes a password easier to type correctly from a printed copy or a screen, but it very slightly reduces the character pool and therefore the entropy per character. For passwords stored in a manager and never hand-typed, leaving this option off gives marginally stronger output.
